title: The Ask, Received
subtitle: A visualisation of making the raise conversation and having it land well
excerpt: A five-minute visualisation of making the ask for a raise, a higher rate, or better terms — and having it received. You picture the moment after the words are said: the room intact, the conversation continuing, the number standing.

practice.whenToUse: Use before a raise conversation, negotiation, or rate-setting discussion — to rehearse saying the number calmly and letting it stand.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation of the ask made and received. You picture the conversation after the words are said: the room intact, the number standing, the exchange continuing without the catastrophe the fear promised.

Picture the setting — a meeting room, a phone call, an email just sent. You are there, or your message has just arrived. The ask has been made. You have said the number clearly, without the hedge. Now you are in the pause.

Notice that the room is still intact. The relationship has not ended. The number you said is still the number — it has not been taken back or apologised for. The other person is considering. You are present, not contracting.
The conversation continues. Whatever the response, you are still there, in the room, with the ask already made. The worst thing has already happened. The ask is in the room. And it was survivable.
Open your eyes when ready. Write one sentence about what the received ask would feel like. That is what you are practicing toward.
